title: RPYR-0019 — `os.posix_spawn` binds argv/setsigdef/setsigmask as eager `ArgIterable` and materializes before validating, so an infinite iterable OOM-aborts the VM (SIGABRT) where CPython rejects it in O(1) — and it needs no privilege (posix.rs:1403/1409/1417)
one_line_repro: import os, itertools; os.posix_spawn('/bin/true', map(str, itertools.count()), os.environ)   # SIGABRT (CPython: TypeError in ~0.1s)
prior_art: Same class as RPYR-0011 (math.sumprod) and the RPYR-0012..0016 eager-collect-parity gaps (fuzzer RUSTPY-0016 = posix.setgroups). NEW sites: posix_spawn argv/setsigdef/setsigmask. Crucially UNPRIVILEGED (unlike setgroups, which needs root), so more weaponizable. No specific RustPython issue.
found_by: eager-collect-parity (informed explore — 3 new gaps in #[derive(FromArgs)] struct PosixSpawnArgs, invisible to the scanner; reproduced live)
toolkit_class: eager-collect-parity (unbounded eager materialization vs CPython O(1) reject/stream, Class G)
cpython_behavior: argv: CPython rejects a non-list/tuple in O(1) (`TypeError: posix_spawn: argv must be a tuple or list`) — a generator/map never materializes. setsigdef/setsigmask: CPython validates each element as it iterates against a fixed-size sigset, raising `ValueError: signal number ... out of range` at the first bad element (O(1) memory). RustPython collects the whole iterable first, then validates → an infinite input balloons to OOM → SIGABRT.
guarded_twin: IN-FILE: the sibling `execv`/`execve` in the same posix.rs type argv as `Either<PyListRef, PyTupleRef>` — an O(1) type reject, exactly CPython's argv contract. posix_spawn uses `ArgIterable` instead. Fix argv by switching to `Either<PyListRef,PyTupleRef>`.
notes: `PosixSpawnArgs` (a `#[derive(FromArgs)]` struct — invisible to the eager-collect scanner) binds argv as `ArgIterable<OsPath>` and setsigdef/setsigmask as `ArgIterable<i32>`, each fully materialized before validation. Confirmed differential (cheap): a finite generator argv is ACCEPTED and materialized by RustPython (`os.posix_spawn('/bin/true',(str(x) for x in range(3)),env)` runs) while CPython raises `TypeError: argv must be a tuple or list` immediately. Agent-reproduced the OOM: `os.posix_spawn('/bin/true', map(str, itertools.count()), {})` balloons RustPython to SIGABRT; CPython O(1). posix_spawn needs no privilege (cf. RPYR-0016/RUSTPY-0016 setgroups → root), so it is the more weaponizable member. FIX: argv -> `Either<PyListRef,PyTupleRef>` (matches the in-file execv/execve twin); setsigdef/setsigmask -> validate while streaming (bounded sigset) instead of collect-then-check. Class J (both-balloon) posix_spawn `file_actions` correctly filtered OUT.
